comments.body:
I triaged this as a `create-action` request.

I’ll prepare a workflow proposal to auto-report failed agent runs to GitHub with configurable enable/disable controls.

Reply with:

```text
@sepo-agent /approve req-7a70e4
```

to start the action creation workflow.

<!-- sepo-agent-request base64:_XCIifQ -->

@sepo-agent /answer Yeah it's more or less like when people install our app in their own repository, we should be able to collect and gather their bugs in this repo and we should be able to launch fixes

Yes, that matches the intent.

- When users install the app in their own repos, failed agent runs should report back to a central intake in this repo.
- Reports should include enough context to act quickly (`source repo`, `run URL`, error summary, fingerprint for dedupe).
- Default can be enabled for public repos, with a clear opt-out toggle.
- From each intake report here, maintainers should be able to launch a fix flow back to the source repo.

One decision to confirm before implementation: should the central intake be **GitHub Discussions** (as in the original issue) or **Issues**?

I agree. Use **Discussions as the default intake**, then manually promote only actionable reports into Issues.

That gives us a cleaner flow:

1. Failed runs from installed repos post structured reports into a dedicated Discussion category, e.g. `Agent Run Reports`.
2. Reports are grouped/deduped by fingerprint so repeated failures do not flood the repo.
3. Maintainers inspect the discussion thread and promote confirmed bugs into Issues when they are actionable.
4. The promoted Issue becomes the place to track the fix and launch repair work against the source repo.

I would avoid creating Issues automatically at first. Auto-created Issues are useful only after we have strong dedupe, severity classification, and enough confidence that the report is product-actionable. Discussions are better for noisy telemetry and triage.

Suggested default:

- Public installed repos: auto-report failed agent runs to Discussions by default.
- Private repos: opt-in, because logs may contain sensitive context.
- Maintainers can disable reporting with config.
- Only maintainers or trusted automation promote Discussion reports into Issues.

So the implementation target should stay close to the original issue: **auto-report failures to GitHub Discussions, not Issues, with a later manual promotion path into Issues.**

Yes, I’d use that category:

`https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/discussions/categories/bug-report`

I’d organize it as **one Discussion per distinct bug fingerprint**, not one giant thread and not one Issue per failure.

Concrete shape:

- Category: `Bug Report`
- Title format: `[agent-failure] owner/repo route failed: <short error/fingerprint>`
- Body includes: source repo, workflow run URL, route, trigger kind, target URL, commit SHA, failure phase, sanitized error summary, fingerprint, first seen time
- Follow-up comments include repeat occurrences: latest run URL, count, last seen time, any changed stack/error details
- Manual promotion: when a maintainer decides it is actionable, create an Issue from the Discussion and link both ways
- Fix launch: the promoted Issue becomes the place to run `/implement` or another repair workflow

This matches the repo’s current architecture pretty well. There are already helpers for creating Discussions and validating categories in [`discussion.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/discussion.ts), plus a CLI for creating a discussion from a generated markdown body in [`create-discussion.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/create-discussion.ts). The existing action template currently talks about optional Issue reporting in [`agent-action-template.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/action-templates/agent-action-template.yml), so I’d change the new design toward Discussion reporting instead of Issue reporting.

Implementation plan I’d suggest:

1. Add a config variable like `AGENT_FAILURE_REPORT_DISCUSSION_CATEGORY`, defaulting to `Bug Report`.
2. Add a failure-report body formatter with a stable fingerprint.
3. Add a CLI/workflow step that checks the category exists before posting.
4. If a discussion with the same fingerprint already exists, comment there.
5. Otherwise create a new Discussion in `Bug Report`.
6. Document the manual promote-to-Issue flow.
7. Keep auto-created Issues out of v1.

I would only create Issues automatically later if we have good dedupe and severity rules. For now, Discussions are the right intake surface.

Sepo adopted this issue as a sub-orchestrator child of #107.

| Parent issue | Stage | Parent round | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| #107 | bug-report-intake | 3 | Running |

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `delegate_issue` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected child issue delegation: Child #27 completed with PR #116 reviewed as SHIP. Continue the #107 umbrella plan with the next clear high-priority remaining workstream, #26 bug-report intake; #94 was explicitly skipped by the requester and #62/#23 is already represented by the base onboarding PR stack.

title: Automatically report issues on GitHub
